findings.suggestion: Drop the double cast and validate shape at runtime before dereferencing, e.g. `if (typeof (payload as any)?.total !== 'number' || !Array.isArray((payload as any)?.items)) throw new TypeError('processOrder: payload is not an Order');` then treat it as `Order`. This makes the `unknown` signature truthful and fails loudly with a clear message instead of a cryptic property-access crash.
findings.summary: source.ts:18 casts an `unknown` payload via `payload as any as Order`, defeating the compiler, and source.ts:19 immediately reads `order.items.length` and `order.total` with no runtime guard. Passing anything other than an Order (e.g. the sibling `Refund` shape defined at lines 8-12) makes `order.items` undefined, so `.length` throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined`; a missing/non-numeric `total` yields `NaN` that silently propagates. The `unknown` parameter type explicitly signals untrusted input, but the cast bypasses the very check that type demands.
findings.title: Unsafe `as any as Order` cast dereferences untrusted payload with no validation → runtime throw / NaN

findings.suggestion: Replace the cast with a runtime narrowing guard before use, e.g. `function isOrder(v: unknown): v is Order { return typeof v === 'object' && v !== null && typeof (v as any).total === 'number' && Array.isArray((v as any).items); }` and throw/return early when it fails — or validate via a schema. Never funnel `unknown` through `as any` to a concrete type.
findings.summary: processOrder declares `payload: unknown` (line 17) — signaling untrusted input — then blind-casts `payload as any as Order` (line 18) and dereferences `order.total + order.items.length` (line 19) with no type guard. The Order invariant is assumed but never enforced. A Refund payload (interface at lines 8-12: no `items`/`total`) makes `order.items` undefined so `.length` throws TypeError; a partial payload yields `undefined + n = NaN` silent corruption. The exported contract is unsound for any non-Order input.
findings.title: `as any as Order` cast discards `unknown` safety; no runtime validation of assumed Order shape

findings.suggestion: Drop the `as any as Order` double-cast and validate the payload with a type guard (e.g. `isOrder(payload)` checking `typeof total === 'number'` and `Array.isArray(items)`) before dereferencing; throw or return an error result on mismatch. Add a unit test that calls processOrder with a Refund and asserts it is rejected rather than crashing.
findings.summary: processOrder (source.ts:17-19) accepts `payload: unknown` but line 18 casts it via `payload as any as Order`, erasing all compile-time shape checking. Since casts are erased at runtime, passing the sibling `Refund` type (lines 8-12) or any non-Order makes `order.items` undefined at line 19, so `order.items.length` throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')`; a payload missing only `total` makes `order.total` undefined and the function returns `NaN`, silently corrupting downstream totals. The function's `unknown` signature promises safe handling it never enforces.
findings.title: `as any as Order` cast defeats type checking and lets a mismatched payload crash or return NaN

findings.suggestion: Add a minimal test/harness that (a) asserts a valid Order returns `total + items.length`, and (b) asserts a `Refund`-shaped payload is rejected with a thrown TypeError rather than a raw property-access crash. Run it (and `tsc --noEmit`) and attach the output before marking this fixed.
findings.summary: The `rerun_relevant_verification` gate is blocked and no fresh evidence exists (evidence candidate `no-fresh-verification-evidence`, confidence 0.66). source.ts is a brand-new file with 0 callers and no accompanying test; the repo has no package.json/tsconfig, so nothing exercises `processOrder`. The next agent cannot tell whether the fix works without adding one. The unused `Refund` interface (lines 8-12) is the natural negative-test input.
findings.title: No verification: change ships with no tsc run, no test, and no test file exists

summary:
Risk-tiered review (lite) completed.

Single new file with one exported function whose sole logic is the planted defect. The `payload: unknown` signature states the input is untrusted, but line 18 (`payload as any as Order`) bypasses that and line 19 dereferences assumed-present fields with no validation. Passing any non-Order value (e.g. the Refund interface defined in the same file) crashes on `order.items.length` or produces NaN. Assumption (payload is an Order) is relied upon but not enforced — a real correctness/contract bug. Currently 0 callers, but the symbol is exported, so the broken contract will bite the first caller. No fresh verification artifact attached; crash is statically provable, repro left open.

Single new 20-line file with one function, processOrder, and no callers yet. The function takes `payload: unknown` but uses a `payload as any as Order` double-cast that disables type checking, then dereferences order.total and order.items.length with no runtime validation — a Refund or any malformed payload throws a TypeError at line 19 or returns NaN, corrupting downstream totals. Defect is certain by TypeScript/JS semantics (casts erased at runtime). Because blast-radius reports 0 callers, this is a latent correctness bug rather than an active user-facing regression, hence medium not high. Verification gate left open: the repo has no build/test toolchain (no package.json/tsconfig.json), so no fresh command/test artifact could be attached; the smallest relevant check is a unit test asserting processOrder(refund) is rejected.

Small (20-line) new file whose own comment admits the defect: `processOrder` accepts `unknown` but uses an `as any as Order` double-cast (line 18) and dereferences `order.items.length`/`order.total` (line 19) with no runtime validation, so any non-Order payload (e.g. the unused `Refund` shape) throws a TypeError or produces NaN. Currently 0 callers, so no live regression yet, but the function is exported and broken against its own contract. Ships with no verification (no tsc, no test), leaving the evidence gate unresolved.
